user.title: Connect Emby server
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to your local Emby server. The API key is stored in Home Assistant and redacted from diagnostics.

data_description.name: Display name of the integration; recommended: EMBi
data_description.host: Local IP address or hostname without http:// or https://
data_description.port: Default: 8096 for HTTP or 8920 for HTTPS
data_description.ssl: Enable only when the local Emby endpoint uses HTTPS
data_description.api_key: Emby API key used for playback, discovery, and explicitly confirmed maintenance actions

reconfigure.title: Edit connection
reconfigure.description: Change the server address, port, HTTPS setting, or API key. The stored API key is never displayed. Leave the field empty to keep it. EMBi reloads after saving.

data_description.name: Display name of the integration
data_description.host: Local IP address or hostname without protocol prefix
data_description.port: Port of the local Emby endpoint
data_description.ssl: Enable only when the endpoint uses HTTPS
data_description.api_key: Enter a replacement key only when it should change. Leave empty to keep the stored key.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.

abort.already_configured: This Emby server is already configured.
abort.unique_id_mismatch: The new connection belongs to another Emby server.

init.title: Manage EMBi
init.description:
Configure several pages in one session. Normal settings remain an unsaved draft until final apply. Destructive actions are hidden while the draft is dirty.

{draft_summary}

clients.title: Media players and devices
clients.description: App-specific rules affect only one ReportedDeviceId.AppName identity. Device-wide rules affect every current and future app variant of the exact ReportedDeviceId. Unresolved legacy rules are preserved until you deliberately remove them.

data_description.client_mode: Active playback only exposes clients only while playing or paused.
data_description.allowed_device_ids: Exact client/app identities used only in Selected devices only mode.
data_description.ignored_player_keys: Blocks only the selected app variant.
data_description.ignored_reported_device_ids: Blocks all app variants of the exact device.
data_description.unresolved_ignored_ids: Values that could not be classified safely during migration. Deselect only when you deliberately want to remove the old rule.

clients_bulk.title: Bulk device selection
clients_bulk.description: Bulk actions update only the current draft and do not reload EMBi.

clients_allow_all.title: Select all current app variants
clients_allow_all.description: Adds all {count} unique client/app identities to Devices to display and switches the draft to Selected devices only.

clients_allow_none.title: Clear displayed-device selection
clients_allow_none.description: Clears the draft selection without changing the mode.

clients_ignore_apps_all.title: Ignore all current app variants
clients_ignore_apps_all.description: Adds all {count} unique client/app identities to the app-specific draft rules.

data.confirm_bulk: I understand and confirm this draft change

clients_ignore_devices_all.title: Ignore all current devices
clients_ignore_devices_all.description: Adds all {count} unique raw client identities to the device-wide draft rules. All app variants are affected after apply.

data.confirm_bulk: I understand and confirm this draft change

server_cleanup_settings.title: Server cleanup
server_cleanup_settings.description: Enable or disable maintenance and choose the separate manual age threshold. Cleanup uses the normal EMBi connection API key. This page changes only the draft.

data_description.age_preset: The numeric day value remains the stored source of truth.
data_description.custom_age_days: Used only with Custom. Existing custom values such as 364 remain unchanged.
data_description.server_cleanup_enabled: Disabling the master switch also disables automatic cleanup in the draft.

server_auto_cleanup_settings.title: Automatic server cleanup
server_auto_cleanup_settings.description: Configure the automatic draft. New activations start 120 seconds after final apply. Later runs use a persistent absolute next-run time and repeat 24 hours after the previous attempt completes.

data.server_auto_cleanup_remove_ha_entities: Also remove matching Home Assistant media players

data_description.age_preset: The numeric day value remains the stored source of truth.
data_description.server_auto_cleanup_enabled: No run starts before final apply.
data_description.server_auto_cleanup_remove_ha_entities: New activations default to off. Existing stored values are retained. Removing a registry entry can lose a custom name, entity ID, dashboard references, automations, HomeKit, or Siri assignments.

server_auto_cleanup_confirm.title: Really enable automatic cleanup?
server_auto_cleanup_confirm.description: The first run starts 120 seconds after final apply. EMBi then checks every 24 hours. Every sufficiently old record that passes the safety checks can be removed in one run; there is no maximum deletion count. Active players and records without a valid last activity are protected.

data.confirm_auto_cleanup: I understand the effects and want to enable automatic cleanup

ha_cleanup.title: Clean up Home Assistant entries
ha_cleanup.description: Only {count} inactive, revalidated candidates are offered. This action is unavailable while normal settings contain unsaved changes.

data.confirm_cleanup: I confirm removal from the Home Assistant entity registry

data_description.cleanup_entity_ids: Candidates are checked again immediately before removal. Removing a registry entry does not delete an Emby server record.

server_cleanup.title: Run manual server cleanup
server_cleanup.description: {count} records are strictly older than {age_days} days and eligible. {active_count} active players and {unknown_count} records without a valid timestamp are protected. The age comes from the last applied settings.

data.remove_deleted_ha_entities: Also remove matching Home Assistant media players

data_description.delete_device_record_ids: Each item is one exact server history record.
data_description.remove_deleted_ha_entities: Default: off. Registry removal occurs only after server deletion, fresh /Devices revalidation, exact config-entry/platform/unique-ID checks, and an unloaded entity with no state.

server_cleanup_confirm.title: Confirm permanent server cleanup
server_cleanup_confirm.description: You are about to delete {count} exact device records. Enter exactly ‘{phrase}’ and enable the confirmation switch.

data.confirm_delete: I confirm permanent deletion from the Emby server

cleanup_report.title: Last cleanup run

apply.title: Apply changes
apply.description:
Validate and save the complete draft exactly once. EMBi reloads at most once.

{draft_summary}

discard.title: Discard changes
discard.description: Discard every unsaved draft change. Stored options remain unchanged and EMBi is not reloaded.

about.title: About Emby Integration - EMBi
about.description:
EMBi brings Emby out of the YAML corner and tidies up the device graveyard. You decide which media players appear in Home Assistant and which historical device records may really go.

Less device chaos, more control — and before deleting, EMBi would rather ask once too often than once too little.

Version {version} · Project: Seger · Based on Home Assistant Emby and pyemby.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.
error.confirmation_text_mismatch: The confirmation text does not match.
error.selection_required: Select at least one device record.
error.invalid_selection: The submitted selection is no longer valid or has become active. Reopen the dialog and try again.
error.invalid_age: Choose a preset or enter a valid custom number of days.
error.server_cleanup_disabled: Enable server cleanup in the current draft first.
error.storage_failed: The persistent maintenance state could not be saved. No options were applied.
error.auto_requires_cleanup: Automatic cleanup requires the server-cleanup master switch.

abort.server_cleanup_disabled: Server cleanup is disabled in the applied options.
abort.unsaved_changes: Apply or discard the current draft before starting a destructive action.
abort.no_changes: There are no changes to apply.
abort.ha_cleanup_complete: Home Assistant cleanup finished: {removed_count} registry entries removed.
abort.server_cleanup_complete: Server cleanup finished: {server_deleted} server records deleted, {server_failed} failed, {registry_queued} player identities queued, {registry_removed} registry entries already removed. Final follow-up details remain available in Last cleanup run.
